threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'train' and not the 'overground'—everyone here gives you that look when you do.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the driver with a ukulele. Oddly, the whole bus joined in for 'Wonderwall'—felt like a scene from a movie.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was in Neukölln when I saw a guy in a suit buying a döner while juggling his laptop and a skateboard. Only here, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see bars turning their beer taps off during a BVG strike, as if to say, 'No transport, no party.' A weird but telling dance of local life.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they order a burrito and instinctively ask for extra salsa, but still carry around a reusable straw.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo waiting for the 14, adjusting his bow tie as a pigeon crapped on his shoe. Classic SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that locals don’t really hang out in the Plaça del Sol after sunset; it’s more of a morning coffee spot for us.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle with the 'horitzó' in menus – it’s not just a view, it's a linguistic hurdle at cafes.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the buses run on time. Everyone knows it's a gamble; best just to grab a coffee and hope for the best.
Caught a late night 196 home, and the driver had the radio blasting classic garage. Made me miss the 2000s vibes, everyone was in a good mood.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw an old man teaching his parrot to say 'Guten Morgen' as they strolled past the fitness studio, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find clubs that turn into swimming pools during heatwaves. Floating around to techno beats is a true summer experience.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can spot the difference between a burrito from La Taqueria and one from Chipotle in a blind taste test.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 47 last night, casually checking his phone. Either he had a wedding reception to attend or just another Tuesday in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think Gràcia's squares are just for picnics. Locals know they're the real stage for impromptu concerts and spontaneous debates.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their fresh 'Barcelona' T-shirt from a tourist shop, still crisp without a hint of the city’s wear and tear.
